repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem
When building Neovim for WebAssembly using Emscripten, the build fails during luv configuration as indicated in this issue: #37885
This occurs even though LibUV builds successfully and is installed at `.deps/usr/lib/libuv.a` as a WebAssembly compiled library.

## Solution
This PR modifies `cmake.deps/cmake/BuildLuv.cmake` to detect Emscripten builds and explicitly configure CMake's find path by setting `LIBUV_INCLUDE_DIR` and `LIBUV_LIBRARY` to point to the cross compiled LibUV in `.deps/usr/`. It also configures `CMAKE_FIND_ROOT_PATH` to prioritize the dependencies directory and sets `CMAKE_FIND_ROOT_PATH_MODE_LIBRARY` and `CMAKE_FIND_ROOT_PATH_MODE_INCLUDE` to prevent CMake from searching system paths during cross compilation.

comment_data.body:
should this be in a function? that way, we have one place where the "detect emscripten" logic lives.

`cmake/Util.cmake` https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/b47b0caba8586df57778f8cee07e5a9fa9ddb47c/cmake/Util.cmake
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1,3 +1,12 @@
+if(CMAKE_C_COMPILER MATCHES "emcc$" OR CMAKE_C_COMPILER MATCHES "em\\+\\+$" OR DEFINED EMSCRIPTEN)

comment_data.body: please adjust your editor/tooling/AI to always include LF (EOL) at EOF (end of file)
(especially since the proposed snippet in #38224 requires the exact same logic)
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1,3 +1,12 @@
+if(CMAKE_C_COMPILER MATCHES "emcc$" OR CMAKE_C_COMPILER MATCHES "em\\+\\+$" OR DEFINED EMSCRIPTEN)

comment_data.body: This is a good point. Thanks for pointing it out. I will extract the Emscripten detection logic into a reusable function in `cmake/Util.cmake` instead of repeating the same logic in multiple places.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1,3 +1,12 @@
+if(CMAKE_C_COMPILER MATCHES "emcc$" OR CMAKE_C_COMPILER MATCHES "em\\+\\+$" OR DEFINED EMSCRIPTEN)

comment_data.body: please keep the final newline (may need to check settings in your editor or coding agent)

comment_regular_data.body:
Right now, WASM/emscripten is not an officially supported target (see `support.txt`); we could change that, but that should be a deliberate decision as it comes with additional maintenance burden.

(Also be aware that we're currently deliberately not supporting cross-compilation via CMake; this is something we are working on `build.zig` support for. So before investing time here, please look into whether `build.zig` already works for wasm, and if not, what is needed there.)

Thanks for the context. My goal is actually to add WASM as a supported target for Neovim as described in #35567. I am already working currently on `build.zig` support for WASM instead of CMake. If changes to CMake for this purpose are not desired, I am happy to close this PR:)

I think building to WASM with a supported compiler is a much more focused (and hence reasonable) goal  than general cross-compilation, so I wouldn't reject CMake changes _specifically for that target_ out of hand. The question is mostly how much complication that would add _in total_ (i.e., including all changes necessary to get a working WASM distribution). So my preference would be to keep this as _one_ PR collecting all necessary changes to be merged when done (rather than nickel-and-dime-ing this).

Investigating `build.zig` is also valuable since that is what we eventually want to move towards as the build system of choice. The interesting question is whether that already works out of the box (or with much fewer/simpler changes).

> if this is all that's needed I think we should try it

...but also add full instructions to `BUILD.md` (and info to `support.txt`) ;)

Thanks for the suggestion. I agree that having a single PR collecting all changes needed for a working WASM build would likely be better than several small PRs.

This PR change mainly focuses on fixing the specific issue where `luv` cannot locate the cross-compiled `libuv` when building with Emscripten, which currently causes the build to fail. I am working on the build and addressing issues as I encounter them during the process, and I will keep them in a single PR if needed, as suggested.

Regarding `build.zig`, I also investigated the Zig build. While attempting to build `libuv` for `wasm32-emscripten`, I encountered a failure because the target was not supported in the current `build.zig` switch logic. I fixed this by adding support for the Emscripten target so the WASM build can proceed.

I submitted a fix for that here:
https://github.com/allyourcodebase/libuv/pull/9

From this investigation, it seems that the Zig build system may simplify cross target builds like WASM, since the target and sysroot handling are more explicit in the build configuration. As recommended, this could be a good direction to explore further.

> I agree that having a single PR collecting all changes needed for a working WASM build would likely be better than several small PRs.

ok feel free to repurpose this PR and update its title

> ok feel free to repurpose this PR and update its title

I have been working on both CMake and Zig build fixes-exploring both ways. Should I include the Zig build fixes in this PR also?
Best to keep PRs focused. So please make Zig changes in a separate PR.
